skill.name: specdd-plan
skill.description: Plans SpecDD work by resolving scope, authority, risk, and verification before any code is edited.
skill.summary: Planning skill that ensures proper spec chain resolution and write authority before implementation in SpecDD projects.
skill.author: specdd

security_audit.summary: The skill contains a single SKILL.md file (68 lines) with planning instructions for SpecDD projects. The static analyzer flagged 14 instances of 'external_commands' and 1 instance of 'weak cryptographic algorithm', but all are false positives triggered by Markdown inline code formatting (backticks around filenames and references like `.specdd/bootstrap.md` and `specdd-cli`) and natural language text. No executable code, scripts, or network access exists in the skill. The skill instructs the agent not to edit files during planning, further reducing risk. This is a documentation-only skill with no attack surface.

low_findings.title: False positive: Inline code formatting triggers command pattern detection
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 14 instances of 'external_commands' (Ruby/shell backtick execution) and 1 instance of 'weak cryptographic algorithm' in SKILL.md. After manual review, all detections are false positives caused by Markdown backtick syntax used for inline code formatting (e.g., `.specdd/bootstrap.md`, `specdd-cli`, `Must`, `Must not`). No executable code, shell commands, or cryptographic functions exist in this file. The skill is a pure documentation/planning skill with no code execution capability.

content.value_statement: Agents often start editing code before understanding scope or authority. This skill ensures the agent resolves the spec chain, identifies write authority, and documents risks before any implementation begins.
actual_capabilities: Resolves the active bootstrap chain from .specdd/bootstrap.md files
Identifies the effective spec chain for a target file or behavior
Determines write authority by locating the nearest granting spec
Summarizes target scope before any code changes
Documents verification steps and blockers in a plan
Produces a minimal safe implementation path aligned with project specs
limitations: Only works for projects that already have a .specdd/bootstrap.md file
Does not edit files; planning-only by design
Requires the agent to have context of the current project structure
Depends on accurate and up-to-date spec files in the project

use_cases.title: Pre-implementation planning for SpecDD projects
use_cases.description: Use this skill before writing any code in a SpecDD project to resolve the spec chain and write authority.

use_cases.title: Resolving unclear scope or authority
use_cases.description: Use this skill when the agent is uncertain about which specs apply or whether it has write authority for a change.
use_cases.target_user: AI agent developers integrating Claude or Codex

use_cases.title: Risk and verification assessment
use_cases.description: Use this skill to document risks, conflicts, and verification steps before starting implementation work.

prompt_templates.title: Basic planning request
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan the work needed to add a new API endpoint for user authentication in this SpecDD project. Identify the spec chain and write authority before proposing changes.
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a new feature in a SpecDD project

prompt_templates.title: Resolving unclear authority
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to modify the payment processing module but I am not sure which spec grants write authority. Use the specdd-plan skill to resolve the spec chain and clarify what I can and cannot change.

prompt_templates.title: Risk-focused planning
prompt_templates.prompt: Before I refactor the database access layer, use the specdd-plan skill to identify the relevant specs, document the risks, and outline the verification steps required.

prompt_templates.title: Full workflow planning
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the specdd-plan skill to: 1) Resolve the bootstrap chain, 2) Walk the spec chain for the target files, 3) Identify write authority, 4) List files likely to change, 5) Document verification needs, and 6) Summarize the smallest safe implementation path.
prompt_templates.scenario: Complex multi-file change requiring full spec compliance

output_examples.input: Plan the work to add a new field to the User model in this SpecDD project.
output: Target scope: User model schema extension in src/models/user.py
Files likely to change: src/models/user.py, migrations/0042_add_user_field.py, tests/test_user_model.py
Verification: Run schema validation tests, migration dry-run, and model unit tests
Risks: Backward compatibility with existing user records; new field must be nullable or have a default value

output_examples.input: I need to change the API rate limiting behavior but I am not sure if I have authority.
output: Authority status: UNCLEAR - no spec explicitly grants write authority for rate limiting
Recommendation: Do not proceed. Request clarification from the project owner or check if a newer bootstrap.local.md defines this scope.
Blockers: Need explicit spec grant before any edit can be authorized

best_practices: Always read the bootstrap chain in the documented order before planning any work
Never infer write authority from filenames, conventions, or similar symbols
Reread the narrowest relevant spec when you need exact text for a decision, not the entire chain
Document risks, conflicts, and verification steps explicitly in the plan output
anti_patterns: Do not start editing files before resolving the spec chain and write authority
Do not infer authority from similar filenames or module names without explicit spec support
Do not propose broad refactors or new contracts unless the active specs or user request require them

faq.question: What is SpecDD?
faq.answer: SpecDD is a specification-driven development methodology where all code changes must be authorized by explicit specs. Projects following SpecDD have a .specdd/bootstrap.md file at the root.

faq.question: Does this skill edit files?
faq.answer: No. The specdd-plan skill is planning-only. It instructs the agent to resolve scope and authority, then produce a plan. Implementation is a separate phase.

faq.question: What if my project does not have .specdd/bootstrap.md?
faq.answer: This skill will not work. Use the specdd-adopt skill instead to add SpecDD to a new or existing non-SpecDD project.

faq.question: How does the skill determine write authority?
faq.answer: The agent walks the spec chain from the content root to the target and identifies the nearest local spec that explicitly grants write authority for the intended change.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as listed in its supported tools.

faq.question: Why does the skill tell the agent not to reread the entire spec chain?
faq.answer: To save context and avoid stale information. The agent should reread only the narrowest relevant spec when exact text is needed for a decision.
